title: Intake questionnaire is presented before syllabus generation begins
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has no active course
steps: Student submits a course creation request for topic 'Introduction to Rust'
Observe the agent module response before any syllabus is generated
expected_result: The agent returns a structured questionnaire collecting knowledge level, learning objectives, and availability. No syllabus content is returned in this response.

title: Syllabus generation is blocked when questionnaire has not been completed
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
The student has no active course
The intake questionnaire has not been submitted
steps: Student sends a direct request to generate a syllabus without first completing the questionnaire
expected_result: The agent rejects the syllabus generation request and returns an error indicating that the intake questionnaire must be completed first.

title: Questionnaire with empty knowledge level field is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated
steps: Student submits the intake questionnaire with the knowledge_level field left blank
Observe the agent module validation response
expected_result: The agent returns a validation error identifying knowledge_level as a required field and does not proceed to syllabus generation.

title: Grade distribution matches point weights defined in approved syllabus
preconditions: A student has completed the intake questionnaire
The agent has generated a syllabus specifying 60% homework and 40% final project
The student has approved the syllabus
steps: Retrieve the grade distribution record created for the course
Compare homework weight and final project weight against syllabus values
expected_result: The grade distribution record shows homework_weight=60 and final_project_weight=40, matching the approved syllabus exactly.

title: Grade distribution is not derived from a fixed institutional default
preconditions: Two students have approved different syllabi: Student A with 70% homework and Student B with 50% homework
steps: Retrieve the grade distribution for Student A's course
Retrieve the grade distribution for Student B's course
Compare the two distributions
expected_result: Student A's grade distribution shows homework_weight=70 and Student B's shows homework_weight=50, confirming distributions are syllabus-driven and not fixed.

title: Grade distribution record is absent before syllabus approval
preconditions: A student has a generated syllabus in pending-approval state
steps: Query the grade distribution record for the student's course
expected_result: No grade distribution record exists for the course. The system returns an empty result or 404, confirming distribution is not created until approval.

title: Content generation pipeline starts immediately after syllabus approval
preconditions: A student has a syllabus in pending-approval state
The content generation pipeline is observable via status events
steps: Student approves the syllabus
Poll the pipeline status endpoint for the course
expected_result: Within the observable polling window the pipeline status transitions to an in-progress state and at least one lesson generation event is recorded.

title: Content generation does not start before syllabus approval
preconditions: A student has a syllabus in pending-approval state
steps: Query the pipeline status for the course without approving the syllabus
expected_result: The pipeline status is not in-progress. No lesson or homework generation events exist for the course.

title: All lessons and homework are included in the generation trigger
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus containing exactly 3 lessons and 3 homework assignments
steps: Wait for the content generation pipeline to complete
Count lesson records and homework records created in the database
expected_result: Exactly 3 lesson records and 3 homework records are present in the database, confirming all content types were generated.

title: Agent prompts for consent before using content library material
preconditions: The content library contains existing material relevant to the student's approved syllabus topic
A student has approved a syllabus
steps: Begin content generation for the course
Observe the agent interaction for a consent prompt before library content is accessed
expected_result: The agent emits a consent prompt to the student before accessing or deriving from content library material. Generation is paused pending the student's response.

title: Content library material is not used when student denies consent
preconditions: The content library contains existing material relevant to the student's approved syllabus topic
A student has approved a syllabus and has been prompted for library reuse consent
steps: Student responds to the consent prompt with denial
Allow content generation to complete
Inspect generated content for references to library source identifiers
expected_result: Generated content contains no references to content library source identifiers. All content is freshly generated.

title: Content library material is reused when student grants consent
preconditions: The content library contains existing material relevant to the student's approved syllabus topic
A student has approved a syllabus and has been prompted for library reuse consent
steps: Student responds to the consent prompt with approval
Allow content generation to complete
Inspect the generated content metadata for library reuse markers
expected_result: Generated content metadata includes at least one library reuse marker referencing the consented source, confirming library content was incorporated.

title: Internet search queries are issued during content generation
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus
The agent module is configured with a search tool integration
Search query logs are accessible
steps: Trigger content generation for the course
Allow at least one lesson to be generated
Retrieve the search query log for this generation run
expected_result: At least one internet search query is recorded in the search query log associated with the generation run, confirming external sources were consulted.

title: Content generation run with no search tool configured does not proceed silently
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus
The agent module search tool integration is disabled or misconfigured
steps: Attempt to trigger content generation for the course
Observe the agent startup response
expected_result: The agent returns an error or warning indicating that the internet search tool is unavailable and content generation does not start.

title: Search queries are topic-relevant and not generic
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus for topic 'Distributed Systems'
Search query logs are accessible
steps: Trigger content generation for the course
Retrieve the search query log after the first lesson is generated
Inspect query strings for topic relevance
expected_result: All recorded search queries contain terms related to 'Distributed Systems' or its subtopics, confirming queries are scoped to lesson content.

title: Pipeline status events are emitted for each generation stage
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus
The pipeline status stream endpoint is accessible
steps: Subscribe to the pipeline status stream for the course
Approve the syllabus to trigger generation
Collect all status events until the pipeline reaches a terminal state
expected_result: At least one status event is received for each named pipeline stage (e.g., lesson generation, homework generation, review). Each event includes a stage name and a status value.

title: No status events are emitted before generation is triggered
preconditions: A student has a syllabus in pending-approval state
The pipeline status stream endpoint is accessible
steps: Subscribe to the pipeline status stream for the course
Wait 5 seconds without approving the syllabus
Collect any status events received
expected_result: Zero status events are received during the observation window, confirming the pipeline does not emit status before generation is triggered.

title: Status events are delivered in pipeline stage order
preconditions: A student has approved a syllabus with at least 2 lessons
The pipeline status stream endpoint is accessible
steps: Subscribe to the pipeline status stream
Allow generation to complete
Record the sequence of stage names in the received events
expected_result: Status events arrive in the documented pipeline stage order. No later stage appears before an earlier stage in the received sequence.
